Full job description
JOB TITLE: Operations Manager

REPORTS TO: Owner

Role Purpose

To represent the HR Hospitality brand and adopt a first-class approach across the guest base to maximise sales, revenue and operational standards. To provide effective leadership, management and budgetary control of the business and hospitality operation, and be accountable for achieving KPIs and objectives as agreed with the Owner. To maintain and develop strong guest relations at operational level at all times and identify opportunities for business growth.

Areas of Responsibility & Key Accountabilities

1. Driving Performance

Accountable for the achievement of specific KPIs agreed with your line manager in line with company strategy.
Key operational lead on all strategic initiatives, maintenance, labour scheduling, margin management, operating standards and compliance measures.
Interpret and analyse monthly management accounts and P&Ls. Benchmark performance and implement corrective measures and best practice.
Deliver projects on time, within budget, and fully embedded with designated owners.
Fully support and drive annual operational and marketing activities in line with set KPIs to increase turnover and profit.
Manage your team in line with agreed company processes and communicate effectively across all departments.
2. Guest & Competition

Ensure effective and timely communication of information to the General Manager, Head Chef and support teams.
Consistently carry out full venue walk rounds, highlighting areas requiring improvement from a team, operational and guest perspective.
Learn from internal and external guest feedback and ensure actions are implemented where appropriate.
Design and support the sharing of best practices and operational synergies.
3. Lead, Drive and Inspire

Lead, motivate and develop the General Manager and management teams to live company values, achieve objectives and deliver personal development goals.
Responsible for the day-to-day management of the General Manager(s) and/or management teams to maximise efficiency, operational standards and service levels – covering people, tasks, standards and behaviours.
Improve team quality and retention through effective recruitment, training, development and coaching.
Contribute to and support the implementation of the business plan in line with strategy and agreed KPIs.
Actively engage support teams to work in partnership to drive excellence across all areas.
Identify and support training needs and develop plans accordingly.
4. Processes & Compliance

Act as a brand custodian and ensure brand integrity is protected at all times through adherence to standards across Operations, HR, Marketing and Maintenance.
Support the execution of all compliance measures across the business.
Drive implementation of company policies and procedures.
Analyse available data to identify improvements and drive a culture of continuous improvement.
Role Dimensions

Financial (budgetary control etc.)

Non-financial (people management etc.)

Delivery of agreed targets/measures and project timelines

Direct reports Via scheduled performance reviews in line with job specifications

Typical Outputs (reporting requirements etc.)

Delivery of business plans
Producing statistical information regarding guest services as required
Communication, implementation and execution of the marketing calendar
Providing operational process information as required
Financial budget cycle reporting
Person Specification

Competency Requirements

Commercial and Financial AcumenApplies logical, well-developed ideas and solutions to issues. Considers the wider picture and value/cost benefits when making decisions.

Planning & Monitoring
Uses systems in place to plan and monitor work. Plans and monitors direct reports' work ensuring proactive support and guidance is provided.

Right First Time Delivery, Drive and OwnershipEnjoys responsibility and delivers against agreed targets. Works with pace, focus and optimism to overcome obstacles and deliver commercial solutions. Encourages others to remain positive.

Influencing with Impact
Presents strong arguments with passion, conviction and commercial credibility. Has the ability to prioritise and manage team and guest expectations.

Leadership
Actively encourages initiative, open communication and team engagement. Delegates appropriately and provides development opportunities. Provides constructive feedback, coaches others and addresses poor performance.

Team Player
Demonstrates company values, engages team members, treats everyone fairly, explains rather than directs, celebrates success and supports others.

Integrated Working
Builds effective working relationships across the business to ensure targets are delivered.

Technical / Professional Expertise Required

Previous experience working in a fast-paced, changeable environment.
Competent user of IT systems including Excel, Word and email systems.
Proven track record in a comparable role.
Ability to lead, mentor and coach others.
Excellent communication skills.
Strong relationship building skills.
Strong business and financial acumen.
Excellent attention to detail.
Self-motivated, ambitious and proactive self-starter.
Pay: £45,000.00-£51,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Company pension
Discounted or free food
Employee discount
Health & wellbeing programme
On-site parking
Private medical insurance
Work Location: In person

shortRoleReason: Senior operations role covering performance, people, compliance and business growth.
companySummary: Rudyard Hotel is a hospitality business focused on guest experience, operational standards and revenue growth.
roleSummary: The Operations Manager will lead day-to-day operations, manage performance and budgets, support the management team, and drive standards, compliance and growth.
headlineAttraction: Lead operations, shape performance and drive growth in a hospitality business.

advertSummary: Operations Manager role at Rudyard Hotel in Leek, offering £45,000-£51,000 full-time and in person. The role combines operational leadership, financial control, team development, compliance and guest experience improvement.
personalisedIntro: I’ve led operations in fast-moving environments where service, standards and commercial performance all matter at once. This role fits the way I work: close to the detail, clear on the numbers, and comfortable being accountable for day-to-day delivery as well as longer-term improvement.
whyThisRole: This is the kind of role where I can add value quickly because it combines operational control, financial discipline, team leadership and visible standards. I’m used to working across those areas together, not as separate tasks, and I know how much difference that makes in a hospitality setting.

selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve already worked in a role where tighter control, better reporting and stronger cash performance mattered. That gives me a solid base for the financial side of this job, especially where I need to interpret accounts and act on them.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the main proof point for commercial grip and budget control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve helped improve profit, release working capital and strengthen cash performance through tighter operational control.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: leadership in a fast-paced, changeable environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve led day-to-day operations through a scale-up phase, where pace, discipline and resilience were essential. That experience maps well to a role that needs calm control, clear priorities and consistent follow-through.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting evidence for pace, ownership and operational leadership.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led operations through growth while keeping delivery, people and customer requirements under control.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve built practical systems to improve compliance and progression tracking in a safety-critical environment. That’s relevant here because the role needs someone who will protect standards and make compliance workable, not just documented.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as proof of hands-on compliance thinking and process improvement.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve created a simple tracking system that made compliance and progression easier to manage.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My reading of this role is that it needs someone who can hold the operation together day to day, keep a close eye on the numbers, and make sure standards do not slip. It also needs a leader who can work with managers and teams in a practical way, spot issues early, and turn feedback into action.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation, released £33.3k of working capital through a 40.3% reduction in debtor balances, and helped increase cash at bank by £172.6k year on year.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can work with financial information and help improve performance, not just report on it.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led day-to-day operations in a scale-up environment and worked across production, people and systems to keep delivery disciplined.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role needs someone who can develop managers and improve team performance while keeping standards steady.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked through growth and change where priorities shifted quickly and operational resilience mattered every day.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is close to the pace and variety this role appears to demand.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a user input system to monitor ride operating hours and support operator progression in a safety-critical setting.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I’m comfortable putting structure around compliance and making it usable for the team.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve managed and supported a team of 12 through annual business-critical projects while maintaining service standards.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role depends on clear communication across managers, support teams and the owner.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built senior reporting across workflow, room utilisation, payroll and contractual income, including reconciliation of 64 PCSE remittance files covering £2.83m.
experienceMappings.relevance: This supports the reporting and monitoring side of the role, especially where data needs to be turned into action.

focusAreasToBring.title: Financial control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would stay close to the accounts, margin and budget cycle so I can spot pressure early and help turn the numbers into practical action.

focusAreasToBring.title: Standards and compliance
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a visible grip on process, walk rounds and follow-through so standards are maintained consistently, not just when there is a problem.

focusAreasToBring.title: Team performance
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on coaching, clear expectations and regular feedback so managers and teams have the support they need to deliver well.

focusAreasToBring.title: Guest feedback into action
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would treat guest feedback as operational input, not just commentary, and make sure it leads to visible improvements.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of operational control, financial discipline and hands-on leadership that fits what this role appears to need. My background spans guest-facing, regulated and change-heavy environments, so I’m comfortable balancing service, standards and commercial pressure at the same time.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months getting close to the numbers, the team and the current operating rhythm. I’d focus on where performance is strongest, where standards are slipping, and where small changes could improve margin, consistency and guest experience. From there, I’d help turn priorities into actions that managers can actually own and deliver.
companyHighlights: Rudyard Hotel is focused on guest experience, operational standards and revenue growth.
The role reports to the Owner and sits close to day-to-day operational control.
The brief places clear emphasis on brand integrity, compliance and continuous improvement.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people are direct, accountable and willing to improve how things are done. The emphasis on open communication, fair treatment and hands-on support feels like a good match for how I lead.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the team, the guest journey, the current KPIs, the budget position and the main pressure points so I can understand what is really happening day to day.
I would focus on reporting, standards, compliance and team routines, making sure there is a clear view of performance and that issues are being followed through properly.
I would work with the Owner, General Manager and wider team to prioritise a small number of improvements that support revenue, margin, service consistency and retention.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring practical control, clear leadership and a steady focus on the numbers, the team and the guest.
closingProofPoints: I’ve helped improve profit and cash performance in a regulated healthcare operation.
I’ve led operations through growth while keeping delivery and standards under control.
I’ve built compliance and reporting tools that made day-to-day control easier for the team.
contentNotes: I kept the company-specific section limited to facts clearly stated in the advert.
I used hospitality-adjacent evidence from leisure, healthcare and manufacturing because the evidence bank does not include direct hotel experience.
I avoided inventing hotel-specific achievements, systems or team structures.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
